data.title: IMF sees signs of US economic strain but lack of data due to shutdown clouds picture
data.description:
Key points:
  • IMF sees Q4 U.S. growth slowing from previous forecast due to government shutdown
  • IMF: tariffs, slowing immigration, policy uncertainty weighing on U.S. activity
  • U.S. government shutdown delays IMF assessment of U.S. economic policies
  • IMF sees G20 progress on debt despite Trump boycott of South Africa summit

By David Lawder

The International Monetary Fund is seeing signs of strain in the U.S. economy with fourth-quarter growth likely decelerating from previous forecasts, but a lack of data due to the government shutdown has clouded its ability to assess U.S. economic performance, IMF spokesperson Julie Kozack said on Thursday.

Kozack told a regular news briefing that the IMF has delayed its annual "Article IV" policy consultations with U.S. authorities because the shutdown prevented preparatory work, with rescheduled timing not yet set.

"The U.S. economy has proven to be resilient in the past few years. We do now see strains starting to mount," Kozack said. "Domestic demand has been moderating, and job growth is slowing. The combination of slowing immigration inflows, tariffs, broader policy uncertainty have been weighing on activity."

She said that there would be a negative impact on U.S. growth in the fourth quarter due to the record 43-day partial government shutdown , and the rate would likely be below the IMF's previous forecast of 1.9% issued in October. Kozack added, however, that this effect would likely be reversed in the first quarter of 2026, in line with the end of previous U.S. government shutdowns.

But the lack of accurate economic data since October 1 "has recently complicated our ability to assess the state of the U.S. economy and to undertake our preliminary work for the Article IV consultation," Kozack said.

She repeated the IMF's view that U.S. inflation is on a path to return to the Federal Reserve's 2% target, but tariffs have increased upside risks to inflation while slowing job growth further complicates the Fed's monetary policy choices.

"So the Fed has appropriately lowered the policy rate in recent months," Kozack said. "We see caution needed going forward as the Fed balances these two factors of sort of upside risk to inflation and downside risk to the job market."

The IMF still views inflation expectations in the U.S. as being "well anchored," she said, but noted that while the rate of price increases has slowed, the higher level of prices "is causing pain in certain segments of society."

Asked about the implications of the Trump administration's boycott of a G20 leaders summit in South Africa on November 21-23, Kozack said that the forum remains an important platform for the world's biggest economies to "pool expertise to solve shared problems." IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva is scheduled to attend the summit after meetings in Angola with government authorities.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent participated in a G20 finance leaders meeting in October during IMF and World Bank annual meetings and helped deliver a joint G20 declaration to keep working on debt vulnerabilities.

"So this was an important outcome of that G20 meeting, and we do expect, going forward, that the topic of debt will remain a priority under the upcoming U.S. G20 presidency," Kozack said.

Washington takes over the G20 presidency in 2026 with a planned leaders summit in Miami.

data.title: SoundThinking Slides After Contract Delays, Cut to Full-Year Outlook
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of SoundThinking slipped after the maker of the ShotSpotter technology cut its full-year revenue outlook due to contract delays.

The stock was down 16%, at $7.09, midday Thursday and has fallen about 46% year-to-date.

The company on Wednesday posted a third-quarter loss of $2 million, or 16 cents a share, compared with a loss of $1.4 million, or 11 cents a share, a year earlier. Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for a loss of 6 cents a share.

Revenue for the quarter was $25.1 million, compared with $26.2 million a year earlier. Analysts were expecting $28 million in sales.

SoundThinking Chief Executive Officer Ralph Clark said lower revenue numbers were due to deployment delays for a handful of contracts, which he said would be fulfilled in the near future. Revenue was also affected after Puerto Rican municipalities declined to renew their ShotSpotter contract in July.

The company cut its full-year revenue outlook to about $104 million, from a previous projection of between $113 million and $111 million.

"While some near-term revenues were delayed by the timing of domestic contracts and an international contract, the underlying strength of our business remains intact," Clark said, adding that the company saw potential in the launch of its AI-powered CrimeTracer Gen3 product, currently in beta release.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Disney's Earnings Had 2 Big Problems. The Stock Is Down. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Walt Disney stock was tumbling on Thursday, after a slump in movie and TV revenue led to the entertainment company missing Wall Street's quarterly sales target.

Shares dropped 8.9% to $106.30 in early trading. The benchmark S&P 500 was 0.5% lower.

The selloff came after Disney reported adjusted earnings of $1.11 a share, as revenue slipped 0.5% from a year ago to $22.46 billion. Analysts were expecting earnings of $1.05 a share on revenue of $22.76 billion, according to a FactSet poll.

Operating income for Disney's entertainment segment plunged 35% from a year ago to $691 million, as both TV advertising and box-office sales fell.

That was offset by an uptick in profit for both streaming and theme parks, which analysts believe will be key to Disney's future earnings growth.

The Disney+ streaming platform didn't take much of a hit from the Jimmy Kimmel controversy, adding 3.8 million subscribers over the quarter, which will be the final time the company discloses these figures. Analysts were expecting 2.2 million additions.

Operating income for the experiences division, which includes Disney's parks, climbed 13% from a year ago to $1.89 billion. Investors should be happy enough with that, considering consumer spending has been slowing and rival Comcast just opened its Universal Epic Universe in Orlando, Disney's traditional backyard.

Disney stood by its previous guidance, which forecasts double-digit adjusted earnings-per-share percentage growth in fiscal 2026 and 2027, but warned that entertainment operating income would likely take a $400 million hit this quarter due to box-office weakness.

Disney channels went dark on YouTube TV last month, after the two sides failed to come to terms on a new streaming agreement. The company said in a 10-K filing that it "cannot predict how long this service blackout will last or reasonably estimate the adverse impact on our results of operations."

As of Wednesday's close, the shares were up 4.8% for the year. The S&P 500 has risen 16% in 2025.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Paraguay Trade Gap Widens in October
data.description:
Paraguay’s trade deficit widened to $191.7 million in October 2025 from $182.9 million a year earlier.

Imports rose 15.2% to $1.71 billion, driven by broad-based increases, particularly in primary goods.

Exports grew 16.7% to $1.52 billion, led by surges in olive oil (175.2%), soy flour (120.5%), and corn (68.7%).

These gains were partly offset by sharp declines in cotton (-33.3%), wheat (-34%), and sugar (-67.8%) shipments.

data.title: S&P 500 Falls 80.85 Points (1.18%)
Nasdaq Composite Falls 439.38 Points (1.88%)
Kinder Morgan Is Maintained at Sector Perform by RBC Capital
DJIA Falls 442.46 Points (0.92%)
Colombia's economic growth expected to accelerate in third quarter
data.description:
By Nelson Bocanegra

Colombia's economy likely accelerated its growth during the third quarter, driven by domestic consumption particularly in entertainment and commerce, despite being dragged by lower mining and oil activity, a Reuters poll showed on Thursday.

The 14-analyst poll pointed to median GDP growth of 3.05% in the third quarter compared to the same period last year, accelerating from the 1.8% reached then and from the 2.1% annual growth registered in the second quarter.

"A recovery is reflected in most economic sectors, driven by a higher level of household consumption," said BTG Pactual.

Projections for the period ranged between 2.8% and 3.3%, close to the country's central bank projection of year-on-year growth for the quarter of 3%.

Meanwhile, growth expectations for Latin America's fourth-largest economy remained unchanged at 2.6% for this year and 2.8% for 2026, the same as in the previous survey. The market estimates GDP growth of 2.9% for 2027.

"Trade imbalance will continue to limit growth, with imports rising and exports affected by the decline in coal and oil," Bancolombia said. "Although consumption is sustaining the expansion, we caution that this foundation is not sustainable in the long term, and therefore, strengthening investment and productivity is necessary."

In quarterly terms, GDP is forecast to have expanded by 1% between July and September compared to the period between April and June.

Economic activity has expanded amid a backdrop of annual inflation of 5.51% at the end of October, above the central bank target rate of 3%, and a tight benchmark interest rate of 9.25%, unchanged since April.

Colombia's national statistics agency DANE will publish its GDP report for the third quarter on November 18.

Last week, Colombia's central bank adjusted its 2025 economic growth forecast to 2.6% from a previous projection of 2.7%, while maintaining its estimate for 2026 at 2.9%.

data.title: Cascale and Sustainable Furnishings Council Engage at Greenbuild 2025
data.description:
Sustainability leaders from around the world gathered at Greenbuild in Los Angeles to explore how data, design, and collaboration can accelerate the shift toward low-carbon built environment

NORTHAMPTON, MA / ACCESS Newswire / November 13, 2025 / Sustainability leaders from around the world gathered at the Greenbuild International Conference & Expo in Los Angeles to explore how data, design, and collaboration can accelerate the shift toward low-carbon, circular, and health-focused built environments.

This year's event focused on the intersection of materials innovation, emissions reduction, and human health - priorities that align closely with Cascale's mission to drive equitable and restorative business practices across the consumer goods industry.

In September, Cascale acquired the tools of the Sustainable Furnishings Council (SFC), the only industry-wide member organization dedicated to advancing sustainability in home furnishings. Rachel Lincoln Sarnoff, Cascale communications director, and Scarlette Tapp, executive director at SFC, attended the conference as well as Greenbuild's Women in Green luncheon.

Sponsored by the Sustainable Forestry Initiative (SFI) - a nonprofit that supports sustainable forest management - the luncheon featured a keynote by Annie Perkins, senior director, green building and supply chain at SFI. It also included a presentation by Jothsna Harris, founder of Change Narrative, who moderated a panel that included Christine Marez, senior vice president, Sustainability & ESG, Cumming Group; Kris Larson, assistant chief of the Los Angeles Fire Department; and Clove Galilee, community engagement coordinator at the Office of Sustainability and the Environment for the City of Santa Monica.

Participation at Greenbuild underscores Cascale's commitment to connecting industries that share similar value-chain challenges and opportunities - from apparel to furniture and beyond. By fostering these cross-sector conversations, Cascale and SFC continue to strengthen collaboration, scale credible measurement, and support innovation that delivers measurable environmental and social impact.

View additional multimedia and more ESG storytelling from Cascale on 3blmedia.com.

Contact Info:

Spokesperson: Cascale

Website: https://www.3blmedia.com/profiles/cascale

Email: info@3blmedia.com

SOURCE: Cascale

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: American Shared Hospital Services Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
American Shared Hospital Services, a leading provider of stereotactic radiosurgery equipment and advanced radiation therapy cancer treatment services, has released its financial results for the third quarter ended September 30, 2025. The company reported a modest increase in revenue and significant growth in EBITDA, driven by its direct patient care services segment.

Financial Highlights

For the third quarter of 2025, American Shared Hospital Services reported a 2.5% increase in revenue, reaching $7.2 million compared to $7.0 million in the same period last year. The company's gross margin improved to 22.1%, up from 19.6% in Q3 2024. Notably, the net loss decreased by 91.8% to $17,000, a significant improvement from the $207,000 loss reported in Q3 2024. Adjusted EBITDA saw a substantial increase of 42.3%, reaching $1.94 million compared to $1.37 million in the previous year.

Business and Operational Highlights

The company's direct patient care services segment showed robust growth, with revenue increasing by 9.4% to $4.0 million. This growth was primarily driven by increased procedures at the new radiation therapy treatment center in Puebla, Mexico. However, revenue from the medical equipment leasing segment decreased by 5.3% to $3.1 million due to lower PBRT volumes.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

American Shared Hospital Services has been actively expanding its footprint. The company signed a 10-year extension with an existing health system for an upgrade to their Gamma Knife System. Additionally, the company is making significant investments in new centers, including a new Gamma Knife center in Guadalajara, Mexico, expected to start operations in the second quarter of 2026. The company also received Certificate of Need approvals for new radiation therapy treatment centers in Bristol and Johnston, Rhode Island.

Management's Perspective

Gary Delanois, Chief Executive Officer, expressed satisfaction with the revenue increases and highlighted the successful ramp-up of new physicians in Rhode Island and the strong start of the new radiation therapy center in Puebla, Mexico. Scott Frech, Chief Financial Officer, emphasized the company's focus on operational enhancements and cost efficiencies, noting the improvement in gross margin and net income. Ray Stachowiak, Executive Chairman, reiterated the company's commitment to long-term growth and shareholder value.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, the company expects continued revenue growth from its new Esprit Gamma Knife center in Guadalajara, Mexico, and further expansion in Rhode Island. The management remains optimistic about the company's strategic initiatives and their potential to drive long-term growth and profitability.

Original SEC Filing: AMERICAN SHARED HOSPITAL SERVICES [ AMS ] - 8-K - Nov. 13, 2025

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Traders Look to Export Sales Release for Chinese Buying — Market Talk
data.description: The USDA says that it will be publishing its daily sales of grains to private exporters that wasn't put out during the 43-day government shutdown. Traders will be watching for this release, in hopes that the USDA says China was a customer for soybeans anytime from October to early November. "We better see China's name on that report," says Brian Pullam of Linn & Associates. Absence of China would likely introduce selling pressure to CBOT grains, particularly soybeans. The USDA also released a schedule to make up for the missed releases through the rest of the year. Soybeans are up 1.2%, corn rises 0.8%, and wheat is up 0.1%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: ACS: Ordinary NPAT up 19.5% and sales up 23.7%, with FY 2025 guidance raised to €820–€855mn
data.description:
Ordinary NPAT grew 19.5% to €585mn and sales rose 23.7% to €36.8bn, driven by strong performance across all segments. FY 2025 NPAT guidance was raised to €820–€855mn, with robust order intake and significant investments in digital infrastructure and M&A.

data.title: Spire Increases Common Stock Dividend >SR
Spire Increases Common Stock Dividend
data.description:
(17:39 GMT) *Spire Increases Common Stock Dividend >SR
(17:39 GMT) Press Release: Spire Increases Common Stock Dividend

23rd Consecutive Year of Increases

ST. LOUIS, Nov. 13, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — The board of directors of Spire Inc. unanimously agreed to increase the annual common stock dividend to $3.30 per share from $3.14 per share. The $0.16 per share increase brings the quarterly rate to $0.825, an increase of 5.1%.

"Spire's dividend increase speaks to the board's confidence in the company's long-term financial expectations and its growth strategy. We believe Spire will continue to be a compelling investment delivering sustainable value to shareholders," said Rob Jones, Spire board chair.

The dividend is payable on January 5, 2026, to shareholders of record on December 11, 2025. Spire has continuously paid a cash dividend since 1946, with 2026 marking the Company's 23(rd) consecutive year of increasing its common stock dividend on an annualized basis. The board of directors also declared the regular quarterly dividend of $0.36875 per depositary share on Spire's 5.90% Series A Cumulative Redeemable Perpetual Preferred Stock, payable February 17, 2026, to holders of record on January 26, 2026.

About Spire

At Spire Inc. we believe energy exists to help make people's lives better. It's a simple idea, but one that's at the heart of our company. Every day we serve 1.7 million homes and businesses making us one of the largest publicly traded natural gas company in the country. We help families and business owners fuel their daily lives through our gas utilities serving Alabama, Mississippi and Missouri. Our natural gas-related businesses include Spire Marketing and Spire Midstream. We are committed to transforming our business through growing organically, investing in infrastructure and driving continuous improvement. Learn more at SpireEnergy.com.

Investor Contact:

Megan McPhail

Megan.McPhail@SpireEnergy.com

data.title: Pfizer Completes Acquisition of Metsera
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Pfizer closed its acquisition of weight-loss drug startup Metsera in a deal that could be valued at more than $10 billion.

Under the deal, Pfizer paid $65.60 a share in cash up front, and could additionally pay a contingent value right of up to $20.65 a share tied to specific clinical and regulatory milestones, the pharmaceutical company said Thursday.

Metsera is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer, and its shares will stop trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market after market close, Pfizer said.

The deal marks an investment in the future of medicine, according to Pfizer.

"By acquiring Metsera, we are directing our resources toward one of the most impactful and high-growth therapeutic areas and positioning ourselves to define it," Pfizer Chief Executive Albert Bourla said.

The deal follows a heated bidding war with rival Novo Nordisk. In September, Pfizer had agreed to buy Metsera for up to $7.3 billion, which Novo Nordisk topped with an unsolicited bid valued at up to $9 billion.

After Pfizer reached its deal with Metsera last week, Novo Nordisk said it wouldn't increase its offer because of its commitment to financial discipline and shareholder value. The company said it will keep assessing opportunities for business development and acquisition.

Metsera, meanwhile, said it decided to take Pfizer's offer in part because of a call from the Federal Trade Commission warning about potential risks to proceeding with a Novo Nordisk deal.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: REG - Crystal Amber Fund - Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
data.description:
RNS Number : 5050H Crystal Amber Fund Limited 13 November 2025  

13 November 2025

Crystal Amber Fund Limited

("Crystal Amber Fund", the "Company" or the "Fund")

Transaction in Own Shares and

Change to Total Voting Rights

The Board of Crystal Amber Fund Limited announces that, in accordance with the authority granted by shareholders at the Company's Annual General Meeting on 13 December 2024, on 13 November 2025 the Company purchased 25,000 of its own ordinary shares of 1p each ("Ordinary Shares") at a price of £1.50 per share. These shares will be cancelled.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), the schedule at the end of this announcement contains detailed information about the individual purchases made by Winterflood Securities on behalf of the Company as part of the buyback programme.

Total Voting Rights  

Following this transaction, the total number of Ordinary Shares in issue at today's date is 84,623,762 and following this transaction, the total number of Ordinary Shares held as treasury shares by the Company is 21,831,415, (which includes 5,312,653 shares purchased by the Company which are pending cancellation). Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 62,792,347 which may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest or a change to their interest in the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

data.title: BorgWarner Vice President Stefan Demmerle Sells 20,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameDemmerle Stefan
RelationshipVice President
TypeSell
Amount$912,750
SEC FilingForm 4

Stefan Demmerle, Vice President of BorgWarner, sold 20,000 shares of common stock on November 11 and 12, 2025, for a total sale amount of $912,750. The sales included 15,000 shares at a weighted average price of $45.5167 and 5,000 shares at $46.00 per share. Following these transactions, Demmerle directly owns 204,039 shares of BorgWarner.

SEC Filing: BORGWARNER INC [ BWA ] - Form 4 - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: Arcus Announces a Further Increase to Private Placement and Share Consolidation
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

November 13, 2025 – TheNewswire - Vancouver, British Columbia - Arcus Development Group Inc. (TSXV:ADG.H)(“Arcus”)announces that further to its news release of November 4, 2025 andOctober 15, 2025, it will increase the size of its ongoing privateplace up to a maximum of $1,650,000.  Subjectto TSX Venture Exchange acceptance, the increased financing will close concurrently with an Arcusshare consolidation, as described below:

(i)        immediately priorto the closing of the financing, Arcus will complete the consolidationon a 10 existing Arcus common shares for 1 post-consolidation Arcuscommon share.  The issued Arcus share capital following theconsolidation and prior to the closing of the financing will be7,387,807 common shares;

(ii)        the closing ofthe financing will immediately follow the consolidation and willconsist of two separate, but concurrent tranches as set out below:

        (a) the first trancheof the financing will consist of a $500,000 offering ofpost-consolidation units at a price of $0.25 per unit.  Each of theseunits will consist of one Arcus post-consolidation common share andone Arcus share purchase warrant.  Each of these warrants willentitle the holder to purchase one Arcus post-consolidation share fora period of two years at a price of $0.40 per share.  Up to 2,000,000post-consolidation Arcus shares and up to 2,000,000 warrants will beissued under the first tranche offering; and

        (b) the secondtranche of the financing will consist of a $1,150,000 offering ofpost-consolidation units at a price of $0.30 per unit.  Each of theseunits will consist of one Arcus post-consolidation common share andone Arcus share purchase warrant.  Each of these warrants willentitle the holder to purchase one Arcus post-consolidation share fora period of two years at a price of $0.40 per share.  Up to 3,833,333post-consolidation Arcus shares and up to 3,833,333 warrants will beissued under the second tranche offering.

Following the completion of the share consolidation andthe successful closing of both tranches of the financing, Arcus willhave 13,221,140 common shares issued and outstanding.  The proceedsfrom the financings will be used by Arcus as general working capitaland to fund work at its wholly owned Touleary project, located in theWhite Gold District of Yukon Territory.

Arcus will apply to list its common shares on the TSXVenture Exchange as a Tier 2 company concurrently with the shareconsolidation and the financing.

On
behalf of Arcus Development Group Inc.


“Ian J. Talbot”


Ian J. Talbot, President
and CEO


Neither the TSX
Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is
defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts
responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.


For additional information contact:


Ian J. Talbot, President and CEO


Arcus Development Group Inc.


e-mail:             ijtalbot@shaw.ca

Website:         www.arcusdevelopmentgroup.com

data.title: The U.S. shutdown is over, for now.
data.description:
By Jeff Mason

This was originally published in the U.S. politics newsletter, which is issued every Thursday.Sign up here to learn about the latest updates and what they mean in U.S. politics.

The longest-ever government shutdown is over — but both Republicans and Democrats are keeping it alive by casting blame and trying to score points about its causes and its aftermath. Meanwhile, the White House is annoyed that what it saw as a successful shutdown strategy by President Donald Trump has already been overshadowed by the unwelcome return of the Jeffrey Epstein saga. If you thought the end of the shutdown meant a period of less drama, think again.

What were the consequences of the U.S. government closing for 43 days? Let’s make a little list: most federal workers didn’t get paid, flights across the country were delayed, and food insecurity for low-income families rose. So, who wins? Trump, at least in the short term.

The president showed significant political muscle during the more than six weeks that government operations were idled. His Republican allies in Congress held strong, not bending to robust resistance by Democratic lawmakers, whose demands that Washington address concerns about Affordable Care Act subsidies were resonating with the public and their own base. Trump even managed to go abroad to Asia during the shutdown without sustaining criticism that he was leaving the country at a time when it was under significant domestic stress. (Former President Barack Obama canceled a trip to Asia under similar circumstances in 2013.)

"I just want to tell the American people you should not forget this. When we come up to midterms and other things, don’t forget what they’ve done to our country,” Trump said in the Oval Office on Wednesday night, placing blame on Democrats, even though it was a handful of Democrats in the U.S. Senate whose support broke the impasse.

The midterms are a year away. It’s anybody’s guess what voters will be thinking about then. Democratic victories in elections last week, which Trump conceded were fueled in part by frustration with the shutdown, showed weakness for the Republican president on the issues of the cost of living, something he has promised to address.

The White House is responding to those concerns by eyeing changes to Trump’s schedule, which has been focused largely on foreign issues. Expect to see more domestic trips and economy-focused events. What it can’t change is a renewed focus on Trump’s connections to Epstein. House Democrats on Wednesday released emails that they said raised new questions about Trump's ties to the convicted sex offender and how much he knew about Epstein's abuse of underage girls. The White House accused Democrats of trying to smear Trump to distract from the shutdown ending. The president, who regularly takes questions from reporters at Oval Office events, took none on Wednesday after signing the legislation to end the shutdown. Reporters shouted some about Epstein as they were ushered out the door.

It looks likely to be a dramatic winter. As the Epstein story continues and the political blame games intensify, there is a deadline in January for both sides to agree on another deal to fund the government. If they don’t, this could all happen again early next year.

TOP U.S. POLITICS HEADLINES:

  • Exclusive - Democratic-led states are inadvertently sharing drivers' data with ICE, officials say

  • Epstein emails say Trump 'knew about the girls'; new House Democrat pledges file release

  • Mamdani’s New York victory exposes fault lines in Jewish Democratic politics

  • In Trump 2.0, MAGA-aligned influencers and media emerge as the new mainstream

  • Exclusive - US intel found Israeli military lawyers warned there was evidence of Gaza war crimes, former US officials say

POLL OF THE WEEK:

Democrats show more confidence in their party after recent elections

THE VIEW FROM CANADA:

About those strikes in the Caribbean: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, while attending G7 meetings in Canada on Wednesday, pushed back against criticism from some U.S. allies over the legality of the U.S. strikes there, saying Europeans don't get to dictate how Washington defends its national security.

WHAT TO WATCH FOR:

  • November 18 - Trump meets Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman at White House

  • November 19 - U.S.-Saudi investment conference in Washington

THE WHO, WHAT AND WHEN:

  • When will US air travel be back to normal? Not this week

  • What's in the deal to end the longest US government shutdown?

data.title: REG - Imperial Brands PLC - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 5038H Imperial Brands PLC 13 November 2025  

IMPERIAL BRANDS PLC

Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) No. 549300DFVPOB67JL3A42

Transaction in own shares

Imperial Brands PLC (the "Company") announces that on 13 November 2025 it purchased for cancellation the following number of its ordinary shares of 10 pence each pursuant to its GBP 1.45 billion share repurchase programme, details of which were announced on 30 October 2025.

Description of shares: Imperial Brands PLC - ordinary shares of 10 pence

Number of shares repurchased: 151,748

Date of transaction: 13 November 2025

Average price paid per share: GBp 3,204.59

Lowest price paid per share: GBp 3,181.00

Highest price paid per share: GBp 3,218.00

Broker: Morgan Stanley & Co. International Plc ("Morgan Stanley")

The Company intends to cancel these Shares.

All shares were purchased from Morgan Stanley as an on exchange transaction subject to the rules of the London Stock Exchange.

Following the purchase and subsequent settlement and cancellation of these shares, the remaining number of ordinary shares in issue will be 802,310,707 (excluding treasury shares).

The figure of 802,310,707 may be used by shareholders (and others with notification obligations) as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), detailed information about the individual purchases is attached to this announcement.

data.title: Fincantieri, Kayo Sign Agreement For Naval JV In Albania
data.description:
Fincantieri SpA :

  • SIGNS MOU WITH KAYO TO START JV TO BUILD, MAINTAIN MILITARY SHIPS IN ALBANIA

  • JV WILL OPERATE AS PRIME CONTRACTOR FOR SHIPBUILDING AND MAINTENANCE CONTRACTS FOR THE ALBANIAN MARKET

  • JV WILL BE ABLE TO BUILD VESSELS UP TO 80 METERS IN LENGTH AND 800 TONS IN DISPLACEMENT

  • KAYO TO PROVIDE THE PASHALIMAN SHIPYARD IN VLORA, TO INVEST IN ITS INFRASTRUCTURE MODERNIZATION
